untranslatable_reason: Dickey-Fuller test requires statistical regression analysis (calculating λ coefficient, standard error, and comparing against DF distribution critical values) which cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. The test requires: (1) regressing Δy(t) on y(t-1) to derive λ, (2) computing SE(λ), (3) calculating test statistic λ/SE(λ), and (4) comparing against critical values from Dickey-Fuller tables conditional on drift assumptions. None of these statistical computations are available as pre-computed indicators in the allowed list. The backtest engine would need to expose adf_statistic, adf_pvalue, or similar statistical test indicators to operationalize this signal.
translation_notes: This is a statistical hypothesis test for mean reversion, not a price-action or momentum signal. It requires econometric regression capabilities beyond the scope of the allowed indicator library. Recommend implementing as a custom indicator (adf_test_statistic, adf_test_pvalue) or using a dedicated time-series analysis module outside the backtest engine.
